text.title: Strategy Analysis — Change Strategy — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of Strategy Analysis — Change Strategy must enforce.

rule.statement: The change strategy MUST enumerate ≥2 viable solution options. Single-option theatre is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Single-option recommendations bypass cost-risk-time trade-offs and lock the program to one path.

rule.statement: Every option names the transition states between current and future state.
rule.rationale: Without transition states, programs leap from as-is to to-be and discover blocking work mid-flight.

rule.statement: Each option carries an organisational-readiness gap assessment (skills, capacity, culture, infra).
rule.rationale: Options that ignore readiness gaps fail in execution despite scoring well on cost-value.

rule.statement: Each option scores cost / risk / time on a defined rubric.
rule.rationale: Without a common rubric, options become apples-to-oranges and the recommendation is opinion-based.

rule.statement: The strategy names a single decision-maker (sponsor or steering committee chair) and a decision-by date.
rule.rationale: Decisions without forcing functions linger; the deadline is the forcing function.
